title: OOF empirical pit-window density
summary: Pit decisions have strong race/compound/stint timing priors.
rationale: Pit decisions have strong race/compound/stint timing priors. Instead of only raw TyreLife and RaceProgress, estimate where comparable cars historically pit and feed distance-to-window features.
implementation_hint: Using out-of-fold train statistics, compute smoothed positive rates by Race, Year, Compound, Stint, rounded TyreLife, and binned RaceProgress/LapNumber. Add hazard, cumulative hazard, distance to peak hazard, and percentile-of-stint-window features; for test, fit statistics on all train only.
expected_effect: Can add a strong calibrated prior while preserving model freedom to override it with pace and position context.
risk: Random CV may overstate gains if exact races repeat; use grouped race/year validation to confirm robustness.
